facts.text: Amsterdam was founded at the mouth of the Amstel River, where a dam was built to control flooding between 1264 and 1275.
The city’s oldest securities market, the Amsterdam Stock Exchange, opened in 1602 and is the world’s first modern stock exchange.
Vondelpark, the largest park in Amsterdam, sits in Oud-Zuid and is named after 17th‑century author Joost van den Vondel.
Amsterdam hosted the 1928 Summer Olympics and also staged sailing events for the 1920 Antwerp Games at Buiten IJ.
Hortus Botanicus, founded in the early 17th century, is one of the oldest botanical gardens worldwide and grew the coffee plant that sparked global coffee culture.
After World War II, Amsterdam welcomed a diverse influx of religions, including Sephardic Jews, Huguenots, and later Muslim immigrants, reshaping its cultural fabric.
